column1: # Actions: IGNORE (suppress), WARN (report only), FAIL (break build)
# Cache-control headers: Next.js and Vercel manage caching via their own
# mechanisms (CDN, ISR, stale-while-revalidate). ZAP flags these as missing
# but they are set correctly at the edge.
# Timestamp disclosure: Next.js build IDs embed timestamps. Not exploitable.
# Next.js embeds __NEXT_DATA__ JSON in pages which ZAP flags as information
# disclosure. This is by design and contains no sensitive server-side data.

column3: # Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments (Next.js build metadata)

column1: # ZAP identifying the app as a Next.js SPA. Informational only, not a vulnerability.
# Next.js sets no-store on dynamic routes by default. Vercel handles edge caching.
# This is expected behaviour, not a misconfiguration.
# _vercel_sso_nonce is Vercel's own deployment protection cookie, not our session.
# Anti-CSRF tokens: Next.js server actions use built-in CSRF protection.
# ZAP may flag forms that don't use traditional token fields.
